text.title: Policy as Code: OPA, Kyverno, and Admission Control Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for the policy bundle. Each rule maps to a CI-checkable invariant + a known incident pattern.

rule.statement: Every new policy MUST ship with `validationFailureAction: Audit` (Kyverno) or `enforcementAction: dryrun` (Gatekeeper). Promotion to Enforce / deny MUST follow an audit review + remediation of existing violations.
rule.rationale: Enforcement without audit blocks all teams' pending workloads simultaneously — the worst possible failure mode for a security tool. Audit gives the data to triage; only then enforce.

rule.statement: Every policy MUST have a kyverno test (or conftest verify for Rego) fixture set covering at least one PASS and one FAIL resource per rule. Tests MUST run in CI.
rule.rationale: LLM-authored policies routinely use non-existent fields or wrong API versions. Without tests, broken policies reach the cluster silently and either fail open (do nothing) or fail closed (block everything).

rule.statement: Policies MUST reach the cluster via GitOps (ArgoCD app / Flux Kustomization). `kubectl apply` from operator laptops is forbidden in any environment.
rule.rationale: kubectl-apply bypasses PR review, audit trail, and rollback. A GitOps sync loop ensures cluster state always matches the repo and any divergence triggers drift alerts.

rule.statement: All policies MUST live in a versioned Git repo with semver tags on releases. Policy diffs MUST go through PR review.
rule.rationale: Policies are platform-critical code; treating them as one-off YAML drops re-introduces every problem version control was invented to solve.

rule.statement: A `trusted-registries` policy MUST exist AND (for prod) be paired with a `verifyImages` cosign-signature check.
rule.rationale: Trusted-registry alone allows a compromised CI to push a malicious image to the right registry; verifyImages closes that gap by validating the Sigstore signature.

rule.statement: If the Applies If preconditions in AGENTS.md do not all hold, OR any Skip If trigger fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and pick a different one — applying the rules here on a mismatched input produces wrong output.
rule.rationale: Methodology selection errors cascade. The cheapest place to abort is before any rule fires.
